text: tang: Hello. I never thought I’d be sharing a fresh breakup story here. Ah, sorry about that. It was a sad story, but we were too... Hey, that was... Soo! Tang! That was a bit much. Sootang: So it all began a few weeks ago at a get-together with friends. I met someone I really liked. I reached out first, and we agreed to meet up over the weekend. The conversation flowed well, and the vibe was good. He even brought up where to meet and what to eat first, so I felt like he wasn't against the idea of meeting. Up to that point, it was definitely something. Soo Tang: But the day before our big date, he went out drinking with friends and suddenly— he bailed on our plans. What kind of bulls*** is that? (Pfft) Turns out it was drinks with a girl friend, and after flaking on me the day before, he didn’t even apologize. What a total jerk. Hahaha Wait, hold up. Ah... crap. We’re just reading what’s written here. It’s not our opinion. We’re just reading it, guys. Why do I still want things to work out with him? We’re all meeting up again in a few days. What kind of stance should I take when I see him? Give me some killer flirting tips or ways to make him regret it. Go oppas! You’ll be blessed! Ah~ So this isn’t totally over yet— They just missed their first date, right? Exactly. First date... so basically, they weren’t even officially dating. I think it’s like getting to the movie theater... Bought the tickets, but never went inside. So you don’t even know what movie it was. That’s the situation here. Nice analogy~ That was smooth. Sounds like your radio DJ skills are back. Say it in your DJ voice. They got to the theater... But in my view, nothing actually happened yet. So it’s not a full rejection, but not exactly a win either. Just think of it as nothing’s happened yet. But if you bought a bunch of snacks. Popcorn, drinks—the full combo, already paid. But then didn’t go into the theater— because of the other person. That honestly... would make anyone mad. I’ve made up my mind. I’ve made up my mind. Soo Tang! You don’t need to hold on. Just cut ties with someone like that. And find a new person with their head on straight—a decent guy. Someone who looks only at you. It’s hardly easy with someone like Gwan-sik... Why bother... There’s no Gwan-sik. Gwan-sik doesn’t exist in this world. From our perspective, it’s obvious. Just stop. He’s out. Hey, Soo Tang! He’s OUT. OUT! He’s not even friend material. And let me be real with you, Soo Tang. If

I mean, okay, I could let it slide with the perilla leaf thing— That’s just using chopsticks once. It’s just helping hold it down. But shrimp? Peeling shrimp is actually tough. Did they peel my shrimp too? Yes. Well then, I think peeling shrimp for someone else is just being polite. But I would still be curious about this— this friend sitting across the table— Like, what, your hands don’t work...? Are your fingers...? You *******, peel your own shrimp! Crew: Event debate— Is it okay if your partner secretly contacts your friend to plan a surprise for your anniversary? To surprise me? Well, what kind of surprise is it? Like you walk into the room, and it’s “Surprise!” and then— The two of them doing this, together— Surprise~ Surprise~2! I personally think it’s totally fine. I’ve also asked Hyejeong’s sister for help to plan a surprise too. That’s her sister, though. Huh?
Isn’t that even weirder? Wait... no. No..! NO!! How far is this going? I said NOOO!! What matters is who the event is actually for. text: ra. Crew: Ooh... 😮 Let’s go A different kind of dating problem. Daily texts, daily calls— this Korean-style dating, where we have to meet 3 times a week, doesn’t suit me. I even cried once on the phone because I didn’t want to talk anymore. There’s no other reason. The repetitive, chore-like calls just made me feel trapped. It made me cry. Maybe I’m not cut out for dating? Yeah, that’s definitely a different type of a problem How often did you guys meet when you were dating? If we weren’t busy, almost every day. Whenever we could, we met. But I get the “chore-like, repetitive phone calls” part. But isn’t that kind of normal for everyone? I’m the type who just gets to the point. So I kept calls short. Thankfully! Hyejeong was the same way. The phone gets hot after long calls— we both hated that, so we kept things short and to the point. Back then, if you just turned on the air conditioner and stayed nearby... Of course. The temperature adjusts itself for you. Go LG! Woohoo~! They said “Korean-style dating doesn’t suit me”— But I think maybe— It’s more like their dating style just didn’t match this person. Yeah, yeah. I think. There’s a perfect four-character idiom for this person. What is it? I’ll write it down. No, wait. No, no, haha No no no no Come on! Why do we have calligraphy brushes here? Unless this is a mission— Why is this in the office? You’re seriously gonna grind the ink and write? You want me to grind the ink stick? But seriously— what we need to remember is that it’s not just about different dating styles— it’s about how we were raised. Let’s say they met at 23 years old— that means for 23 years, they grew up in different environments, with different parents, different friends— went to different schools, all that. So how can they be the same? It doesn’t mean either of them is wrong. Neither side is wrong. It just means they need to talk. Just say, “Meeting this often is a bit overwhelming for me.” “I need a bit of alone time.” If the other person understands that— that’s someone understanding and thoughtful. But if they don’t understand— and they get angry— then the relationship probably wouldn’t work anyway. If you’re feeling seriously drained— then I have a career recommendation for you. Astronauts. Once you go up... Yeah, right? Even from the moon, one call takes a few minutes to connect. It takes time. Exactly. text: ou. Astronauts. Once you go up... Yeah, right? Even from the moon, one call takes a few minutes to connect. It takes time. Exactly. What’s important is—even among the three of us— how differently we communicate with our wives. Even if we’re on the same tour bus, Tukutz, for example— as soon as we arrive at the airport, he’s on FaceTime. Even in Japan, we have a song called “1 Minute 1 Second”— and it’s literally like that. So— even while walking down the street, he’s video calling. He calls a lot. Super frequently. And Mithra’s kind of in the middle. In the middle. As for me— I barely call at all. Even Hyejeong tries not to contact me when I’m working. And I also don’t want to give her anything to worry about. So I try not to call until everything’s settled. We’re all just different. Ohhh~ You are in the solo zone If being alone is more comfortable, then just stay alone. Whatever feels right is the right answer. And that doesn’t mean you’re incapable of dating.
